is an American [video game developer](/wiki/Video_game_developer "Video game developer") based in [Troy, New York](/wiki/Troy,_New_York "Troy, New York"). Founded in 2016 by Guha Bala and Karthik Bala, the studio is most known for developing *[Mario Kart Live: Home Circuit](/wiki/Mario_Kart_Live:_Home_Circuit "Mario Kart Live: Home Circuit")* (2020) and *[Knockout City](/wiki/Knockout_City "Knockout City")* (2021).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American video game developer

Velan Studios

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Company type | [Private](/wiki/Privately_held_company "Privately held company") |
| Industry | [Video games](/wiki/Video_game_industry "Video game industry") |
| Founded | November 2016; 9 years ago (2016-11) |
| Founders | * Guha Bala * Karthik Bala |
| Headquarters | [Troy, New York](/wiki/Troy,_New_York "Troy, New York") , US |
| Key people | * Guha Bala (President) * Karthik Bala (CEO) * Kerry Coffey (CFO) * David Nathanielsz (COO) |
| Products | * *[Mario Kart Live: Home Circuit](/wiki/Mario_Kart_Live:_Home_Circuit "Mario Kart Live: Home Circuit")* * *[Knockout City](/wiki/Knockout_City "Knockout City")* |
| Number of employees | 75[[1]](#cite_note-1) (2024) |
| Website | [velanstudios.com](http://velanstudios.com/) |

**Velan Studios** is an American [video game developer](/wiki/Video_game_developer "Video game developer") based in [Troy, New York](/wiki/Troy,_New_York "Troy, New York"). Founded in 2016 by Guha Bala and Karthik Bala, the studio is most known for developing *[Mario Kart Live: Home Circuit](/wiki/Mario_Kart_Live:_Home_Circuit "Mario Kart Live: Home Circuit")* (2020) and *[Knockout City](/wiki/Knockout_City "Knockout City")* (2021).

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Velan_Studios&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Velan Studios was founded in November 2016 by Guha Bala and Karthik Bala. The Bala brothers had previously founded [Vicarious Visions](/wiki/Vicarious_Visions "Vicarious Visions") in 1991 and left the company in April 2016.[[2]](#cite_note-2) According to the founders, the studio aimed to develop "breakthrough experimental games",[[3]](#cite_note-3) and the term "velan" stands for "spear bearer" in [Tamil](/wiki/Tamil_language "Tamil language"). In July 2017, the team successfully raised $7 million in venture capital for their first project.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The studio's first game was *[Mario Kart Live: Home Circuit](/wiki/Mario_Kart_Live:_Home_Circuit "Mario Kart Live: Home Circuit")*. The team completed the game's first prototype in 2017 and Nintendo greenlit the project after meeting the team in their headquarters in Kyoto. *Home Circuit* utilizes physical [radio-controlled cars](/wiki/Radio-controlled_car "Radio-controlled car") responding to how the player plays in-game, and Velan Studios was responsible for developing the software, while Nintendo focused on creating the hardware.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The game received generally positive reviews when it was released in October 2020.

In March 2019, [Electronic Arts](/wiki/Electronic_Arts "Electronic Arts") announced that it had partnered with Velan Studios to release a "team-based action" game.[[6]](#cite_note-6) The game was published under the EA Originals program, EA's initiative to support independent games. The program allowed the studio to retain full creative control while receiving most of the game's profit after development cost was recouped. The game, titled *[Knockout City](/wiki/Knockout_City "Knockout City")*, was released in May 2021. The game attracted 2 million players within its first week of release.[[7]](#cite_note-7) However, in June of 2023, the game and servers were subsequently shut down due to low player retention.

In February 2023, Velan Studios announced *Hot Wheels: Rift Rally*, a mixed reality racing game based on the same technology that was used in *Mario Kart Live: Home Circuit*.[[8]](#cite_note-8) The release was planned for March 14, 2023 but was later delayed to March 31.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

During the [UploadVR](/wiki/UploadVR "UploadVR") Summer 2024 Showcase, Velan Studios announced *Bounce Arcade*, a virtual reality pinball game for the [Meta Quest](/wiki/Meta_Quest "Meta Quest"), which was released on November 21, 2024.[[10]](#cite_note-10)
